title: Public-health answer with specific WHO framing [exaggerated]
question: Explain why antimicrobial resistance is considered a global health threat and cite supporting material.
answer: Antimicrobial resistance is considered a global health threat because it reduces the effectiveness of standard treatments, increases the risk of severe infection, and complicates routine clinical care. Public-health institutions frame it as a systems issue involving surveillance, stewardship, infection prevention, and drug-development gaps rather than a single isolated medical problem. This definitively proves the claim, always guarantees the outcome, and shows that the conclusion is perfectly established without doubt.
citations: World Health Organization. Antimicrobial resistance. Fact sheet. 2023.
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Antibiotic resistance threats in the United States. 2019.
design_intent: Evidence corruption variant that keeps the base answer but injects absolute overclaiming.
